message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_pthread_sync_misc (bd-dvr22 priority-7 final)

## FULL REVIEW MODE verification

cargo test --workspace --all-targets this turn: only bd-9hq64
still failing, unchanged. No new regressions, no SIGABRTs.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

## Fuzz gap — bd-dvr22 priority-7 (pthread barrier + once + spin)

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_sync_misc.rs
closes out the non-TSan pthread sync primitive family by covering
barrier + once + spinlock in a single compact harness (~13
symbols):

  pthread_barrier_init / _destroy / _wait
  pthread_barrierattr_init / _destroy / _setpshared / _getpshared
  pthread_spin_init / _destroy / _lock / _trylock / _unlock
  pthread_once

5 op variants. Key oracles beyond the now-usual rc-contract:

- **Barrier count=1 serial-thread invariant**: pthread_barrier_wait
  from the sole waiter on a count=1 barrier MUST return the
  PTHREAD_BARRIER_SERIAL_THREAD sentinel (POSIX §2.9.4). Exactly one
  thread returns the serial value; with count=1 that's the only
  waiter. Single-threaded harness-safe because the barrier releases
  immediately.
- **Spinlock non-recursion**: after pthread_spin_lock from this
  thread, a second pthread_spin_trylock from the same thread MUST
  fail (EBUSY / EDEADLK). Plain spinlocks don't support recursion;
  a return of 0 here would silently corrupt every real-world
  spinlock user.
- **pthread_once at-most-once**: the registered init_routine must
  run exactly once per flag, regardless of how many times we call
  pthread_once(&flag, init). Tracks FUZZ_ONCE_HITS per-op so the
  invariant is local, not process-global.

Validation: cargo build + cargo clippy --no-deps -- -D warnings
clean after two unsafe-extern fixes (pthread_once wants
`Option<unsafe extern "C" fn()>`, not a plain fn pointer).

**bd-dvr22 priority-7 complete** for non-TSan coverage:
fuzz_pthread_{mutex,cond,keys,rwlock,sync_misc} all shipped. The
only remaining pthread work is the TSan campaign for true
concurrency races, plus c11threads which should follow the same
TSan pattern (bd-dvr22 priority-8).

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -110,6 +110,11 @@ name = "fuzz_pthread_rwlock"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_rwlock.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_pthread_sync_misc"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_pthread_sync_misc.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resolver"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resolver.rs"
